name: Museo Nazionale Scienza e Tecnologia Leonardo da Vinci, Milan
concept&appearance: The museum is housed in the historic 16th-century Olivetan Monastery of San Vittore al Corpo, characterized by two large Renaissance cloisters and a monumental structure. The architectural concept bridges the gap between humanistic and scientific culture, utilizing the ancient monastic spaces for core exhibitions while integrating modern industrial pavilions (such as the Air & Water Transport Building and the Rail Transport Building) to house large-scale machinery. The complex represents a dialogue between the Renaissance heritage of Milan and its industrial evolution.
permanent_exhibitions: 1. Leonardo da Vinci Galleries: The world's largest permanent exhibition dedicated to Leonardo da Vinci, featuring over 170 historical models, artworks, and immersive installations exploring his work as an engineer and humanist. 2. Space Area: Showcases the evolution of space exploration with artifacts like a fragment of Moon rock from the Apollo 17 mission, the Vega launcher (model), and satellite technologies. 3. Rail Transport Building: A large pavilion housing steam locomotives (including the GR 552), electric locomotives (E 430), and historical carriages on real tracks, illustrating the history of Italian rail transport. 4. Air & Water Transport Building: Features the 'Ebe' training ship, the bridge of the 'Conte Biancamano' ocean liner, the Luna Rossa AC72 catamaran, and various aircraft including a Macchi MC 205 V. 5. Enrico Toti Submarine (S-506): Located in the outdoor area, this is the first submarine built in Italy after WWII, open for guided interior tours. 6. Energy & Materials: Includes the massive Regina Margherita Thermal Power Station (1895), and sections dedicated to the lifecycle of products, steel, aluminum, and the chemical industry. 7. Communication: Exhibits on the history of telecommunications from telegraphs to modern digital tech. 8. Particle Physics (Extreme): An exhibition dedicated to the research of the infintely small and the work of CERN and INFN. 9. Nutrition (#FoodPeople): Explores the relationship between food, science, and technology.
central_hall: The central distribution space consists of two monumental 16th-century cloisters (Chiostri) surrounded by the museum's galleries. These open-air courtyards feature Renaissance architecture with arched colonnades and serve as the historic heart of the complex, often used for events and providing access to the various exhibition departments.
special_effects_theaters: VR Cinema (A permanent virtual reality theater created in collaboration with Rai Cinema, equipped with VR headsets for immersive short films and documentaries on science and society, accommodating small groups).
science_popularization_activities: The museum features 'i.labs' (Interactive Laboratories) offering hands-on educational workshops in fields like Genetics, Biotechnology, Chemistry, Robotics, and Energy. It also hosts the 'Tinkering Zone' for creative engineering activities, a 'Playlab' dedicated to children aged 3-6, and the CREI (Center for Informal Education) which develops methodology for science education. Guided tours are available for the Enrico Toti submarine and the Leonardo Galleries.
